title: WorkflowTypeDefinition
description: Master type descriptor for a workflow type. The single entry point for all config related to a type — validateConfigs starts here, and platform-config-engine loads all config via this manifest. Without this, workflowType is a free-form string with no authoritative registry. Each config document (workflow-config, routing-config, field-type-registry, etc.) owns its own active:true state. validateConfigs enforces that exactly one active document of each config type exists per (tenantId, workflowType).

workflowType.description: Canonical string identifier for this workflow type (e.g. SETTLEMENT_EXCEPTION, SSI_SETUP_REQUEST). This is the key used on WorkItem and all other config documents. Uppercase with underscores.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the blotter header and config management UI.

description.description: Business description of what this workflow type represents.

parentWorkflowType.description: Optional. If this workflow type is only ever created as a child (e.g. SSI_SETUP_REQUEST is only created by SETTLEMENT_EXCEPTION via CREATE_CHILD_WORKFLOW), declare the parent here. validateConfigs warns if a parent-only type appears as a direct routing target.

active.description: Whether this workflow type is currently active. Inactive types cannot receive new work items.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version. Referenced by WorkItem.configVersionId to pin the config version active at ingestion time.
